reasoning: CVE-2023-43804: Cookie header not stripped on cross-origin redirects. Fix modifies Retry class to strip Cookie header during redirects. Retry.increment() is the core method patched.
CVE-2021-29510: Infinity input to datetime fields causes infinite loop. Fix adds bounds check in get_numeric() to reject infinity values before parsing.
CVE-2025-50182: Browser/Node.js redirect control. has_jspi is a JavaScript/WASM interop utility function unrelated to the HTTP redirect vulnerability fix.
CVE-2026-21441: Decompression-bomb safeguards bypassed on redirects. The redirect() method in the response handler is directly patched to preserve decompression limits across redirects.
CVE-2025-62718: NO_PROXY hostname normalization bypass leading to SSRF. isLoopbackHost is part of the proxy resolution logic directly patched to fix hostname matching.
CVE-2024-37891: Proxy-Authorization header not stripped on cross-origin redirects. Fix modifies Retry class to strip Proxy-Authorization during redirects, similar to CVE-2023-43804.
The connect() method in the connection module may be modified as part of the fix but is not the primary vulnerability-fixing function; the core fix is typically in TLS certificate verification logic.
_init_decoder initializes the response decompression decoder. With 18 functions extracted, this is a tangential helper touched during a large refactor rather than the core security fix.
CVE-2023-45803: Request body not stripped on HTTP 303 redirect. urlopen() is the main entry point where redirect body-stripping logic is enforced.
CVE-2021-23337: Lodash template injection. Ye is a minified identifier from lodash.min.js, not a meaningful function name. Regex captured minified code artifact.
CVE-2023-45857: XSRF-TOKEN cookie leakage. noop is a generic utility function unrelated to the XSRF token fix. With 33 functions extracted, most are noise.
urlopen is the core HTTP method where the vulnerability fix (likely header or redirect handling) is applied.
idna_encode handles internationalized domain name encoding. While modified in the same commit, the primary fix targets IDNA validation, and idna_encode is a helper in that chain.
CVE-2024-21534: ReDoS in argument escaping. escapeArgument contains the vulnerable regex that was patched to prevent catastrophic backtracking.
__init__ is a constructor modified alongside the urlopen fix. It is not the primary vulnerability-fixing function but may store configuration used by the fix.
body-parser qs parser DoS. createQueryParser configures the query string parser with depth/parameter limits that were tightened to fix the vulnerability.

Generic __init__ constructor from a commit with 4 functions. At Low confidence with an ambiguous name, this is likely a false positive from regex matching a class constructor unrelated to the IDNA fix.
validate is a generic pydantic validator method. The vulnerability is in email validation specifically; validate is part of the validation chain but not the specific fix target.
CVE-2024-35195: Credentials leak via netrc on redirects to different hosts. get_netrc_auth is the exact function patched to check host matching before sending credentials.
isEmptyObject is a generic utility. With 33 functions extracted from this advisory, it is not related to the XSRF-TOKEN vulnerability fix.
Xc is a minified identifier from lodash.min.js. Not a meaningful function name; regex captured minified code artifact.
CVE-2022-24999: qs prototype pollution. parseObject is the exact function patched to block __proto__ key injection during query string parsing.
ReDoS in brace-expansion. expand() is the main function containing the vulnerable regex pattern that was fixed.
toFlatObject is a utility for flattening prototype chains. Not related to the XSRF-TOKEN cookie leakage fix. 33 functions extracted = mostly noise.
CVE-2024-56326: Jinja2 sandbox escape via do_reverse filter. The do_reverse function's implementation was patched to prevent calling str.reverse on arbitrary objects.
ReDoS in brace-expansion (different version range). expand() is the main function fixed to prevent catastrophic regex backtracking.
MultiDecoder handles chained content-encoding (e.g. gzip+deflate). It was patched to fix decompression bomb or decode handling vulnerabilities.
CVE-2022-3517: ReDoS in minimatch. partsToRegExp constructs regex from glob parts and was patched to prevent catastrophic backtracking.
Vo is a minified identifier from lodash.min.js. Not a meaningful function name; regex captured a minified code artifact.
Pydantic email validation vulnerability. validate_email is the specific function where the email validation logic was patched to fix the security issue.
